content:
# Acts Chapter 7 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, PDF pages 232-234 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/ACT_007_page_001.jpg, ACT_007_page_002.jpg, ACT_007_page_003.jpg

---

**1** And the high-priest said, "Are these-things so?"

**2** And he said, "Men, brothers and fathers, hear. The God of-glory appeared to-our father Abraham, being in Mesopotamia, before [he] dwelt in Haran,

**3** and said to him, 'Go-out from your land and from your kindred, and come into the land which ever I-shall-show you.'

**4** Then having-gone-out from [the] land of-[the] Chaldeans, he-dwelt in Haran. And from-there, after the death of-his father, he-removed him into this land in which you now dwell.

**5** And he-gave not to-him an-inheritance in it, not-even a-foot's-step, and he-promised to-give it to-him for a-possession, and to-his seed after him, [there] not being to-him a-child.

**6** And God spoke thus, that his seed will-be a-sojourner in a-land foreign, and they-will-enslave them and will-treat-badly [them] four-hundred years.

**7** 'And the nation to-which they-shall-be-enslaved I will-judge,' said God, 'and after these-things they-shall-come-out and shall-serve me in this place.'

**8** And he-gave to-him a-covenant of-circumcision; and so he-begat Isaac and circumcised him on-the day the eighth, and Isaac [begat] Jacob, and Jacob the twelve patriarchs.

**9** And the patriarchs, having-been-jealous [of] Joseph, sold [him] into Egypt; and God was with him,

**10** and delivered him out-of all his afflictions, and gave to-him grace and wisdom before Pharaoh king of-Egypt, and he-appointed him ruling over Egypt and over all his house.

**11** Now there-came a-famine over all Egypt and Canaan, and great affliction, and our fathers were not finding food.

**12** But Jacob, having-heard [that] there-is grain in Egypt, sent-out our fathers first.

**13** And on the second [visit] Joseph was-made-known to-his brothers, and the family of-Joseph became manifest to-Pharaoh.

**14** And Joseph, having-sent, called-for Jacob his father and all the kindred, in souls seventy-five.

**15** And Jacob went-down into Egypt, and he died, he and our fathers,

**16** and they-were-carried-over to Shechem and were-placed in the tomb which Abraham bought for-a-price of-silver from the sons of-Hamor in Shechem.

**17** And as the time of-the promise was-drawing-near which God had-promised to-Abraham, the people grew and were-multiplied in Egypt,

**18** until there-arose another king over Egypt who had not known Joseph.

**19** This-one, having-dealt-craftily-with our race, treated-badly the fathers, [causing] them to-make their infants exposed, so-as not to-be-kept-alive.

**20** In which time Moses was-born and was beautiful to-God; who was-nourished three months in the house of-the father.

**21** And [when] he was-exposed, the daughter of-Pharaoh took-up him and nourished him for-herself for a-son.

**22** And Moses was-educated in all [the] wisdom of-[the] Egyptians, and he-was powerful in his words and works.

**23** And when a-time of-forty years was-being-fulfilled for-him, it-came-up upon his heart to-visit his brothers, the sons of-Israel.

**24** And having-seen a-certain-one being-wronged, he-defended [him] and made vindication for-the-one being-oppressed, having-struck-down the Egyptian.

**25** And he-was-supposing [that] his brothers understand that God through his hand is-giving salvation to-them, but they did not understand.

**26** And on-the following day he-appeared to-them [as they were] fighting, and he-was-urging them to peace, saying, 'Men, you-are brothers; why do-you-wrong one-another?'

**27** But the-one wronging the neighbor thrust-away him, saying, 'Who appointed you [as] ruler and judge over us?

**28** Do you not wish to-kill me in-the-manner-which you-killed the Egyptian yesterday?'

**29** And Moses fled at this word, and became a-sojourner in [the] land of-Midian, where he-begat two sons.

**30** And forty years having-been-fulfilled, there-appeared to-him in the wilderness of-the mount Sinai an-angel in a-flame of-fire of-a-bush.

**31** And Moses, having-seen, was-marveling at-the vision; and [as] he was-approaching to-observe, there-came a-voice of-[the] Lord:

**32** 'I [am] the God of-your fathers, the God of-Abraham and of-Isaac and of-Jacob.' And Moses, having-become trembling, was not daring to-observe.

**33** And the Lord said to-him, 'Loose the sandal of-your feet, for the place on which you-have-stood is ground holy.

**34** Having-seen, I-saw the ill-treatment of-my people, the [one] in Egypt, and their groaning I-heard, and I-came-down to-deliver them; and now come, I-will-send you into Egypt.'

**35** This Moses whom they-denied, saying, 'Who appointed you [as] ruler and judge?' -- this-one God has-sent [as] both ruler and redeemer with [the] hand of-[the] angel, the-one having-appeared to-him in the bush.

**36** This-one led-out them, having-done wonders and signs in [the] land of-Egypt and in [the] Red Sea and in the wilderness [for] forty years.

**37** This is the Moses, the-one having-said to-the sons of-Israel, 'A-prophet God will-raise-up for-you from your brothers as me.'

**38** This is the-one having-been in the assembly in the wilderness with the angel, the-one speaking to-him on the mount Sinai, and [with] our fathers, who received living oracles to-give to-you,

**39** to-whom our fathers were not willing to-become obedient, but thrust-away [him] and were-turned in their hearts to Egypt,

**40** having-said to-Aaron, 'Make for-us gods who will-go-before us; for this Moses, who led-out us from [the] land of-Egypt -- we-know not what has-happened to-him.'

**41** And they-made-a-calf in those days, and brought-up a-sacrifice to-the idol, and were-rejoicing in the works of-their hands.

**42** And God turned and gave-over them to-serve the host of-heaven, just-as it-has-been-written in [the] book of-the prophets: 'Did-you-bring to-me slain-victims and sacrifices [for] forty years in the wilderness, house of-Israel?

**43** And you-took-up the tent of-Moloch and the star of-the god Rephan, the figures which you-made to-worship them; and I-will-remove you beyond Babylon.'

**44** The tent of-the testimony was among-our fathers in the wilderness, just-as the-one speaking to-Moses ordered, to-make it according-to the pattern which he-had-seen.

**45** Which also our fathers, having-received-in-succession, brought-in with Joshua into the possession of-the nations whom God drove-out from [the] face of-our fathers, until the days of-David,

**46** who found grace before God and asked to-find a-dwelling for-the house of-Jacob.

**47** But Solomon built for-him a-house.

**48** But the Most-High does not dwell in [things] made-by-hands, just-as the prophet says,

**49** 'Heaven [is] my throne, and the earth [is] a-footstool of-my feet. What kind-of house will-you-build for-me, says [the] Lord, or what [is] the place of-my rest?

**50** Did not my hand make all these-things?'

**51** Stiff-necked and uncircumcised in-hearts and in-ears, you always against the Holy Spirit fall -- as your fathers, also you!

**52** Which of-the prophets did your fathers not persecute? And they-killed the-ones having-announced-beforehand concerning the coming of-the Righteous-One, of-whom now you have-become betrayers and murderers,

**53** who received the law at ordinances of-angels and did not keep [it]."

**54** And hearing these-things, they-were-being-cut-to their hearts, and they-were-gnashing the teeth at him.

**55** But being full of-[the] Holy Spirit, having-gazed-intently into heaven, he-saw [the] glory of-God and Jesus standing at [the] right [hand] of-God,

**56** and he-said, "Behold, I-see the heavens having-been-opened and the Son of-Man standing at [the] right [hand] of-God."

**57** And having-cried-out with-a-voice loud, they-held-together their ears, and rushed with-one-mind upon him,

**58** and having-cast [him] outside the city, they-were-stoning [him]. And the witnesses laid-aside their garments at the feet of-a-young-man being-called Saul.

**59** And they-were-stoning Stephen, [who was] calling-upon and saying, "Lord Jesus, receive my spirit."

**60** And having-placed the knees, he-cried with-a-voice loud, "Lord, do not set this sin against them." And having-said this, he-fell-asleep.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **Verse 4 -- "he-removed him"**: Sinaiticus reads μετῴκισεν αὐτόν ("he removed him"), with God as the subject who relocated Abraham. Some later manuscripts clarify the subject differently.

2. **Verse 5 -- "not-even a-foot's-step"**: The Greek βῆμα ποδός is literally "a step of a foot" -- an extremely small unit of land, emphasizing God gave Abraham no tangible inheritance.

3. **Verse 14 -- "seventy-five"**: Sinaiticus reads ἑβδομήκοντα πέντε (75), following the Septuagint count, rather than the Masoretic Text's seventy (70). This is a well-known variant between the LXX and MT traditions.

4. **Verse 16 -- "Shechem"**: Stephen's speech conflates two OT burial traditions -- Jacob's purchase at Shechem (Gen 33:19) and Abraham's purchase of Machpelah (Gen 23:16). Sinaiticus preserves Stephen's speech as delivered.

5. **Verse 20 -- "beautiful to-God"**: The Greek ἀστεῖος τῷ θεῷ is an unusual dative construction meaning "beautiful/fair in God's sight" or possibly "divinely beautiful." Preserved literally.

6. **Verse 33 -- "sandal" (singular)**: Sinaiticus reads τὸ ὑπόδημα (singular "sandal"), whereas the LXX of Exodus 3:5 also uses the singular. Some later manuscripts have the plural.

7. **Verse 37 -- "as me"**: The reading ὡς ἐμέ ("as me") in Sinaiticus follows Deut 18:15 LXX. Some manuscripts read ὡς ἑμέ with slight orthographic variation.

8. **Verse 38 -- "living oracles"**: λόγια ζῶντα -- Stephen describes the Torah as "living utterances," a striking phrase preserved exactly from the manuscript.

9. **Verse 43 -- "beyond Babylon"**: Sinaiticus reads Βαβυλῶνος ("Babylon"), matching the LXX of Amos 5:27. The Hebrew MT of Amos reads "beyond Damascus." Stephen (or the LXX translator) updated the geographical reference to reflect the later Babylonian exile.

10. **Verse 46 -- "house of Jacob"**: Sinaiticus reads τῷ οἴκῳ Ἰακώβ ("for the house of Jacob"). Some important manuscripts (including Vaticanus) read τῷ θεῷ Ἰακώβ ("for the God of Jacob"), following Psalm 132:5 LXX. This is a significant textual variant.

11. **Verse 51 -- "you always against the Holy Spirit fall"**: The Greek word order is preserved literally: ὑμεῖς ἀεὶ τῷ πνεύματι τῷ ἁγίῳ ἀντιπίπτετε. The verb ἀντιπίπτω means "fall against" or "resist."

12. **Verse 53 -- "at ordinances of-angels"**: εἰς διαταγὰς ἀγγέλων -- the preposition εἰς here functions instrumentally, indicating the law was received through angelic mediation (cf. Gal 3:19, Heb 2:2).

13. **Verse 59 -- "calling-upon"**: Sinaiticus reads ἐπικαλούμενον without an explicit object; the context implies Stephen is calling upon [the Lord]. The following direct speech makes this clear.

14. **Verse 60 -- "he-fell-asleep"**: ἐκοιμήθη -- the aorist passive of κοιμάω, a standard early Christian euphemism for death, particularly of martyrs. Luke deliberately uses this gentle term for Stephen's violent death.

-- EMET
